diary:
Woke up early in Issaquah, coffee in hand, did my little mobility routine like always—felt good to start the day on purpose. By the time I rolled into Bellevue, the sunlight was already doing that June thing where everything looks sharper. I grabbed breakfast to-go at Bakery Nouveau, then headed toward the downtown job site near the Bellevue Transit Center. Working on rooftops always has its own rhythm: check the wiring, tweak the panel angles, watch the output climb.

Around lunch I ducked into Elliott Bay Book Company Cafe in my head even though I was in Seattle—book-smell always helps me reset. Later at Home Depot I picked up replacement fasteners and components so I could wrap clean. After work, I drove home quick and ran the Cedar River Trail—sun still up, legs feeling surprisingly steady. Then it was Queen Anne Beer Hall for the USA vs Wales match with friends—bars opened early, and the whole place had that buzz. I’m home again, tired in the best way, like the day actually landed.
thought_process: I tried to keep the morning efficient since the Light Rail closure between Westlake and University District meant more car traffic and unpredictable timing for anyone riding transit—I just stayed flexible with my drive and buffer. The sunny weather at 74F helped me push through installations without rushing, but I still prioritized hydration and safety checks, because a rooftop job punishes shortcuts. I stopped at supply to avoid the classic “almost finished” problem; replacing fasteners on the spot saved time later. The World Cup match at 11am PDT mattered emotionally—downtown bars opening early pulled me toward a more social evening, so I planned my run earlier to keep from feeling sluggish. My mood shifted from focused and steady during the work to lighter and celebratory during the game, then calm again at home as I logged the day.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
